title: Share of adults who are obese vs. daily protein supply
subtitle: Obesity is defined as having a body-mass index (BMI) equal to, or greater than, 30. BMI is a person's weight (in kilograms) divided by their height (in meters) squared.
caption:
Share of adults who are obese vs. daily protein supply, 2022
Obesity is defined as having a body-mass index (BMI) equal to, or greater than, 30. BMI is a person's weight (in kilograms) divided by their height (in meters) squared.

This scatter plot maps the relationship between daily protein supply and adult obesity rates across nations in 2022. The horizontal axis measures daily protein supply in grams per day per capita, ranging from roughly 30 grams to over 140 grams, while the vertical axis displays the percentage of adults with obesity, spanning from 0% to 70%. Points are color-coded by region, distinguishing North America, South America, Africa, Europe, Asia, and Oceania. The data shows a positive association where higher protein supply generally coincides with higher obesity prevalence. In the upper right quadrant, countries with high protein intake and high obesity rates include the United States, Iceland, Ireland, and Serbia, all situated above 130 grams of protein and between 20% and 45% obesity. Pacific Island nations such as Nauru, Tonga, and Samoa occupy the highest positions on the vertical axis, with obesity rates exceeding 60% despite varying protein supply levels between 100 and 125 grams. Conversely, the lower left section contains countries with low protein supply and low obesity rates, including Burundi, Somalia, and Bangladesh, which cluster below 80 grams of protein and under 10% obesity. Vietnam and Nepal also appear in this lower range with protein supplies near 90 grams and obesity rates below 5%. European nations like France, Austria, and Belgium show moderate to high protein supplies around 100 to 120 grams but maintain lower obesity rates between 15% and 25% compared to North American counterparts. The distribution places the global average point near 100 grams of protein and just under 20% obesity. The visible pattern aligns higher daily protein availability with increased percentages of adult obesity across the plotted dataset.
